rationale: Keep this pair because the cited paper appears plausibly foundational to the citing paper’s governance-alignment logic: the cited study tests investment–governance strategic fit and performance contingencies, while the citing study tests channel-usage × governance contingencies on financial performance in B2B settings. Given this strong construct-level overlap (governance fit/alignment, contingent performance effects) and only reference-list-level evidence, it is not safe to treat as an obvious mention-only citation.
